title: Build Complete User Notification System
description: Build a complete notification system feature from scratch. This includes: database schema migrations, backend API endpoints for subscribing/unsubscribing, notification queue processor, email/push notification handlers, and React components for the UI. This is a complex, multi-domain feature that requires Overseer-level decomposition and coordination across multiple agent specialties.

setup_files.requirements.md:
# User Notification System Requirements

## Feature Overview
Build a notification system that:
- Allows users to subscribe/unsubscribe from notification types
- Queues notifications from various sources (payments, messages, comments)
- Sends via multiple channels (email, in-app, push)
- Tracks delivery status
- Respects user preferences and do-not-disturb settings

## Acceptance Criteria

### Database Schema
- [ ] `notifications` table with type, user_id, content, status
- [ ] `notification_preferences` table with user_id, type, enabled
- [ ] `notification_channels` table with email, push, in_app preferences
- [ ] `notification_queue` table for job processing
- [ ] `notification_log` table for audit trail

### API Endpoints
- [ ] `POST /api/notifications/preferences` - Update preferences
- [ ] `GET /api/notifications/preferences` - Get user preferences
- [ ] `GET /api/notifications` - List notifications (paginated)
- [ ] `POST /api/notifications/:id/read` - Mark as read
- [ ] `DELETE /api/notifications/:id` - Delete notification
- [ ] `POST /api/notifications/subscribe/:type` - Subscribe to type
- [ ] `POST /api/notifications/unsubscribe/:type` - Unsubscribe from type

### Notification Types
- [ ] Payment (user made a payment)
- [ ] Comment (someone commented on user's post)
- [ ] Message (new direct message)
- [ ] System (maintenance, security alerts)
- [ ] Weekly digest (summary of activity)

### Channel Handlers
- [ ] Email handler (via SendGrid or similar)
- [ ] Push notification handler (Firebase Cloud Messaging)
- [ ] In-app notification handler (real-time via WebSocket)

### Frontend Components
- [ ] NotificationBell component (with unread count)
- [ ] NotificationDropdown (recent notifications)
- [ ] NotificationCenter (full history and preferences)
- [ ] PreferencesModal (manage notification settings)

### Queue Processing
- [ ] Bull/BullMQ job queue
- [ ] Retry logic for failed notifications
- [ ] Rate limiting (max 10 emails per hour per user)
- [ ] Do-not-disturb honor (no emails 10pm-8am)
- [ ] Batch daily digests

## Technical Stack
- Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- Queue: Bull (Redis-backed)
- Email: SendGrid
- Push: Firebase Cloud Messaging
- Frontend: React + TailwindCSS
- Backend: Node.js/Express

## Success Metrics
- All endpoints tested (E2E + unit)
- 95%+ delivery rate
- < 100ms API response time
- 0 duplicate notifications
- User preferences enforced

├── backend/

└── README.md

## Implementation Phases
1. Database schema (SupabaseConnector)
2. Backend API endpoints (CodeGen Elite - multi-file)
3. Queue processor (CodeGen Pro)
4. Notification handlers (CodeGen Pro - email/push)
5. Frontend components (CodeGen Pro - React)
6. Testing (Test Generator)
7. Integration testing (Overseer verification)

verification.command: ls -1 /tmp/migrations/ | grep -q notification && ls -1 /tmp/routes/ | grep -q notification && ls -1 /tmp/handlers/ | grep -q handler && echo 'PASS' || echo 'FAIL'
